danger_signals.visual_form: Manifestation of creatures from alternate dimensions entering the mortal realm
danger_signals.meaning: World has been fundamentally altered by the Disjunction; reality is compromised and unstable

danger_signals.visual_form: Concentration of hostile creatures in specific zones throughout the game world
danger_signals.meaning: Dimensional rift has permitted malevolent beings to establish territorial control

danger_signals.visual_form: Religious authority figures and their iconography dominating urban areas
danger_signals.meaning: Authoritarian religious control; danger from institutional persecution

danger_signals.visual_form: Military formations and naval war preparation visible in Barcelona setting
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent large-scale conflict and territorial invasion threat

safety_signals.visual_form: Distinct faction strongholds with recognizable architecture and symbols
safety_signals.meaning: Safe haven allied with player's chosen faction

safety_signals.visual_form: Identifiable Renaissance-era figures (Leonardo da Vinci, Galileo, Shakespeare) in positions of influence
safety_signals.meaning: Stable centers of knowledge, culture, and established authority

safety_signals.visual_form: Barcelona and other developed urban centers with civilian population
safety_signals.meaning: Civilization and established order persist despite dimensional corruption

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Crusader military fortifications and medieval knight architecture

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Religious authority symbols and ecclesiastical heraldry
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Religious institutional buildings, persecution symbolism, authoritarian structures

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Islamic crusader iconography and Saracen heraldry
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Eastern military aesthetics, Islamic architectural elements

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Magical practitioner symbols and mystical iconography
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Arcane sanctuaries, magical artifact repositories, dimensional crossing points

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Post-Disjunction reality (magic now exists in mortal realm)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Supernatural creatures, magical phenomena, dimensional instability made visible throughout environment
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs and factions respond to magical threats; gameplay emphasizes supernatural danger management

world_state_to_visual.world_state: 1588 alternate history (Renaissance period with magical corruption)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: 16th-century Barcelona setting merged with demonic and elemental anomalies; historical figures coexist with magical threats
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Historical figures (da Vinci, Galileo, Cervantes, Shakespeare) conduct activities despite supernatural danger

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Dimensional rift remains unstable (not yet permanently opened)
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Ongoing manifestation of creatures and magic, but world not yet consumed; controlled chaos rather than apocalypse
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Four factions actively compete to control or manipulate the rift; player character inherits responsibility to prevent permanent opening

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Religious institutional control visible in city layouts, NPC behavior, authority symbols throughout Barcelona
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Persecution, surveillance, and authoritarian faction mechanics; religious authority structures restrict player agency

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Military readiness iconography, Armada-related fortifications, war preparation infrastructure
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Geopolitical tension affects faction dynamics and quest availability

environmental_storytelling.example: Each of four factions (Templars, Inquisition, Knights of Saladin, Wielders) controls distinct geographical and architectural spaces that communicate their power and ideology without exposition
Monster-infested areas show where dimensional rift corruption is strongest; density and type of creatures indicates proximity to reality tears

Leonardo da Vinci, Galileo, Shakespeare, Machiavelli, and Miguel de Cervantes appear as NPCs in positions reflecting their influence and knowledge domains, grounding supernatural events in Renaissance intellectual landscape
Inquisition's visual presence (architectural style, symbols, uniformed enforcers) in Barcelona communicates extent of authoritarian control without dialogue
Mixing of 16th-century historical Barcelona with dimensional rift phenomena creates visual narrative of reality fracture

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Active in world due to Disjunction
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Territorial hostility; establish zones of control; represent danger to established order
Military organization; crusader ideology; direct combat approach to supernatural threats

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Authoritarian religious power seeking to consolidate control
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Persecution of magical practitioners; institutional control over populace; use of religious authority to manage supernatural threat

entity_behavioral_tells.state: Eastern military faction opposing Christian crusaders
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Alternative ideological opposition to Templar dominance; competing claim to rift control
Embrace of magical knowledge; attempt to understand and manipulate rift; represent academic/arcane approach to supernatural crisis
Must align with one faction; gains special abilities tied to Spiritkind selection; positioned as potential resolver of reality fracture

notes: Lionheart uses alternate history and dimensional corruption as core world-state manifestation vectors. Visual communication relies heavily on faction symbols and territorial control to signal political alignment. The Spiritkind system (demonic, elemental, or bestial spirits) creates player character visual/behavioral differentiation. Isometric 3/4 camera angle provides overhead perspective ideal for reading environmental faction markers and territorial danger zones. Wikipedia source limited to plot and gameplay mechanics; specific visual asset descriptions (color palettes, exact iconography) not available in source material—these remain qualitative descriptors based on historical precedent and genre conventions. The game's layering of Renaissance historical figures with supernatural danger creates dual narrative streams communicated through environmental placement.
sources_used: wikipedia: Lionheart: Legacy of the Crusader
